summary: Socure reported Q1 2026 ARR exceeding $340M with 62% YoY profitable growth, confirming identity verification market consolidation around well-capitalized incumbents.
Q2 Holdings reportedly uses an AI coding tool cutting platform builds from weeks to days, and is collaborating with Amazon on digital banking modernization.
Plaid shipped a proprietary transaction foundation model targeting categorization quality and intelligent finance insights at scale.
Plaid Protect fraud network claims 59% more fraud caught with no added friction, setting a network-effect benchmark single-FI deployments cannot match.
Plaid expanded its Perplexity integration to cover spending tracking, net worth, and AI planning, creating a consumer-facing AI insight layer.
Plaid rebuilt its Income verification engine with finer taxonomy and configurable calculation, upgrading income verification capabilities.
Personetics launched an MCP server enabling agentic AI on customer financial data; also announced a deposit-growth collaboration with Atomic.
Nymbus launched a secure MCP server for AI-driven core banking actions, adding agentic access infrastructure to its core banking platform.
Jack Henry's 2026-03-26 8-K disclosed entry into and termination of material agreements plus a new financial obligation; counterparty unknown.
FinCEN and OFAC jointly proposed rules requiring payment stablecoin issuers to comply with the GENIUS Act, establishing the first federal AML/sanctions framework for stablecoin rails.
Bud Financial launched an MCP server enabling AI agents to access bank-grade financial intelligence, entering agentic-data-access infrastructure market.
Alkami's 8-K disclosed a material definitive agreement and an unregistered equity sale; details limited.
